title: Validate structured output, then repair within budget
description: Parse model JSON against a strict schema and run a bounded repair loop on failure rather than trusting the first parse.
body: Use when a call must return machine-consumable structure (JSON, function arguments, enums). Do not JSON.parse and use the result directly, and avoid unbounded re-prompting on malformed output. Actions: define a strict schema; validate every response; on failure feed the validator error back for at most N repair attempts; fall back to a typed error after the budget. Validation signals include a conformance test over sampled responses and a test that malformed output triggers repair then a clean fallback. Risk boundary: never silently coerce or drop fields to force a parse; surface the failure and deny the downstream action.
